draft.introduction: Psalm 34 is an acrostic thanksgiving psalm attributed to David after his escape from Abimelech. It combines personal testimony with wisdom instruction, inviting the community to “taste and see that the Lord is good.”

translation.text: I will bless the Lord at all times; his praise shall continually be in my mouth. Oh, taste and see that the Lord is good! Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him. The angel of the Lord encamps around those who fear him, and delivers them.
translation.original_text: εὐλογήσω τὸν Κύριον ἐν παντὶ καιρῷ διὰ παντὸς ἡ αἴνεσις αὐτοῦ ἐν τῷ στόματί μου

translator_notes.note: The imperative “taste and see” (γεύσασθε καὶ ἴδετε) became central to eucharistic theology. The Church Fathers read it as an invitation to experiential knowledge of God through the sacraments, not merely intellectual assent.
